title: Multiply with 3 and 6
essential_question: What strategies can you use to multiply with 3 and 6?
strategies: To multiply by 6: double the ×3 fact

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.3 'Multiply with 3 and 6' pp.147-150

pretest.question: To find 6 × 7, double 3 × 7. What is 3 × 7? Then double it.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.3 'Multiply with 3 and 6' pp.147-150
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic E & Module 3 — Multiplying with Units of 3 and 6

learn.title: Multiplying by 3
learn.content: Skip count by 3: 3, 6, 9, 12, 15, 18, 21, 24, 27, 30. Or add the number three times: 3 × 4 = 4 + 4 + 4 = 12.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.3 'Multiply with 3 and 6' pp.147-150
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic E & Module 3 — Multiplying with Units of 3 and 6
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Multiplying by 6: Double the ×3 Fact
learn.content: Since 6 = 2 × 3, you can multiply by 3 first, then double. 6 × 5 → 3 × 5 = 15, then double 15 = 30. So 6 × 5 = 30.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.3 'Multiply with 3 and 6' pp.147-150
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic E & Module 3 — Multiplying with Units of 3 and 6
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Skip Counting by 6s
learn.content: You can also skip count by 6: 6, 12, 18, 24, 30, 36, 42, 48, 54, 60. Notice: every ×6 product is exactly double the matching ×3 product.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.3 'Multiply with 3 and 6' pp.147-150
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic E & Module 3 — Multiplying with Units of 3 and 6
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Arrays Show ×3 and ×6
learn.content: An array of 3 rows × 5 columns has 15 dots (3 × 5). An array of 6 rows × 5 columns has 30 dots (6 × 5). The ×6 array is just two ×3 arrays stacked — that's why 6 × 5 is double 3 × 5.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.3 'Multiply with 3 and 6' pp.147-150
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic E & Module 3 — Multiplying with Units of 3 and 6
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Split 6 Into 3 + 3
learn.content: Another way to find ×6: split 6 into 3 + 3 and add. 6 × 7 = 3 × 7 + 3 × 7 = 21 + 21 = 42. This works because 6 groups of 7 is the same as two sets of 3 groups of 7.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.3 'Multiply with 3 and 6' pp.147-150
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic E & Module 3 — Multiplying with Units of 3 and 6
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: ×3 — 손가락 추적으로 미스카운트 방지
learn.content: ×3 사실은 건너뛰기 셈으로 풀지만, 6번~9번 점프에서 자주 미스카운트. 방지법: 손가락 하나에 한 번 점프 — 손가락 개수 = 다른 인자. 예: 3 × 7 → 손가락 7개 펴고 '3, 6, 9, 12, 15, 18, 21' (각 손가락마다). 검증: 손가락 다 폈을 때의 마지막 값이 답. 흔한 실수: 한 번 더 (4×8=27 대신 24) 또는 한 번 덜 (3×7=18 대신 21).

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.3 'Multiply with 3 and 6' pp.147-150

practice_r2.question: True or False: 6 × 4 equals 3 × 4 plus 3 × 4.
hints: So 6 fours equals two sets of 3 fours.

feedback.correct: Right — splitting 6 into 3 + 3 keeps the product the same.

practice_r2.feedback_correct: Right — splitting 6 into 3 + 3 keeps the product the same.

practice_r2.question: Find the difference: 803 − 416.
practice_r2.explanation: 803−416: 3−6 borrow → 13−6=7; tens 0−1 borrow → 10−2=8 (after lending); hundreds 7−4=3. Result: 387.
hints: Borrow across the zero in tens.
